offer.what: Low-ticket books ($3–$7), bookazines, and Bible-study/spiritual-warfare AI chatbots (via Poe) — all equipping Christians to use AI for ministry, discipleship, and daily life. Supplemented by a free community and a free newsletter.
offer.coreOutcome: Christians confidently integrate AI into their faith walk and ministry without compromising spiritual integrity.
offer.pricePoint: $3–$7 per book or bookazine; chatbots free via Poe; community and newsletter free
offer.commitment: One-off purchases; no visible paid recurring subscription
leadMagnets: Free AI chatbots on Poe (Arch Angel Michael War Room, Logos Bible Study AI, Ephah Bible Study Tool)

audience.icp: Evangelical/Pentecostal Christians — pastors, ministry leaders, Christian students, and devout laypeople — who sense that AI is reshaping their world but fear it conflicts with their faith or don't know where to start.
painPoints: Fear that AI is spiritually dangerous or anti-Christian
Feeling left behind by rapid technological change
Uncertainty about how to use AI for ministry or discipleship without losing biblical grounding
Personal spiritual battles (addiction, sexual temptation) they want faith-based help with
Concern that AI will replace human theological wisdom and pastoral care
segments: Ministry leaders and pastors seeking theological frameworks for AI adoption
Christian students and professionals cautious about embracing AI tools
Believers dealing with personal sin struggles (sexual temptation/pornography)
Families wanting to protect themselves and their finances from AI-driven disruption

salesCycle.trial: Free chatbots via Poe; free newsletter and community serve as entry points
salesCycle.closeMechanism: Self-serve: direct buy button on book and bookazine pages
salesCycle.topObjection: "AI is spiritually dangerous / at odds with my faith."

ahaMoment: First chapter of a book or first chatbot interaction where the subscriber experiences faith and AI working together — realizing AI can amplify rather than threaten their walk with God.

retention.recurring: Digital Discipleship newsletter (free) and free community. No paid recurring product visible.
retention.expansion: Cross-sell from one book to another; upsell from free chatbot usage to paid books, bookazines, or deeper community involvement
retention.churnRisk: One-off buyers with no recurring hook are easy to lose; newsletter subscribers churn if content is inconsistent, too promotional, or fails to deliver fresh spiritual+practical value

lifecycleGaps: Welcome & Onboarding sequence for new subscribers and buyers
Lead-Magnet Nurture for newsletter/community sign-ups who haven't purchased
Post-purchase cross-sell sequence (one book buyer → other books, bookazines, chatbots)
Win-Back / Re-Engagement for inactive newsletter subscribers
Consistent value newsletter cadence (existing 'Digital Discipleship' quality and frequency unknown)
toneVoice: Prophetic-meets-practical: spiritually charged language (Scripture, faith affirmations, Kingdom vocabulary) blended with clear, empowering action steps. Warm, bold, and encouraging — like a Spirit-filled pastor who is also a tech-savvy innovator. Avoids corporate or cold tone.
compliance: Standard CAN-SPAM and GDPR. No HIPAA or financial compliance needs. Religious content requires doctrinal sensitivity — avoid divisive theological positions and respect diverse Christian traditions.
